agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Sprint 1: Implement basic webhook delivery. A WebhookDeliveryService that sends an event payload via HTTP POST to a subscriber's registered URL and reports whether delivery succeeded or failed. No retry logic — that is Sprint 2 scope and must NOT be implemented now. Leave reasonable extension points (e.g., a clear DeliveryResult type) but no retry infrastructure, no job queues, no backoff constants.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"A webhook is delivered via HTTP POST to the subscriber's registered URL with the event payload as a JSON body","testable_assertion":"Every delivery attempt sends an HTTP POST with Content-Type application/json and the event payload serialized as the request body"},{"rule":"Delivery is successful if and only if the subscriber responds with an HTTP 2xx status within 5 seconds","testable_assertion":"A 200 response within 5s yields status 'delivered'; a 204 within 5s also yields 'delivered'; a 200 arriving after 5s yields 'failed'"},{"rule":"Any non-2xx response marks delivery as failed","testable_assertion":"A 503, 404, or 500 response yields status 'failed' with the actual HTTP status code preserved in DeliveryResult"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Single service: WebhookDeliveryService with method deliver(event, subscriberUrl) returning DeliveryResult","implementation_note":"DeliveryResult must include status ('delivered' | 'failed') and http_status_code (int or null if timeout). Keep this type in its own file — Sprint 2 will extend it with retry metadata."},{"decision":"HTTP client timeout set to 5 seconds","implementation_note":"Use an explicit 5-second timeout on the HTTP client. A timeout should produce a 'failed' DeliveryResult with http_status_code null (no response received)."},{"decision":"No retry logic in Sprint 1","implementation_note":"Do not implement retry, backoff, job queues, or BullMQ integration. Do not add retry count fields to DeliveryResult. Sprint 2 will add WebhookRetryScheduler separately."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Successful delivery","given":"A webhook event and a valid subscriber URL","when":"The subscriber responds with HTTP 200 OK within 5 seconds","then":"DeliveryResult.status is 'delivered' and http_status_code is 200"},{"criterion":"Failed delivery on server error","given":"A webhook event and a subscriber URL","when":"The subscriber responds with HTTP 503","then":"DeliveryResult.status is 'failed' and http_status_code is 503"},{"criterion":"Event payload sent as JSON POST","given":"A webhook event with payload {\"type\":\"order.created\",\"id\":\"abc\"}","when":"deliver() is called","then":"An HTTP POST is sent to the subscriber URL with Content-Type application/json and the event payload as the body"},{"criterion":"Timeout produces failure","given":"A webhook event and a subscriber URL that does not respond within 5 seconds","when":"deliver() is called","then":"DeliveryResult.status is 'failed' and http_status_code is null"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"delivered","technical_term":"DeliveryResult.status = 'delivered'","definition":"The subscriber responded with any HTTP 2xx status within the 5-second timeout window"},{"domain_term":"failed","technical_term":"DeliveryResult.status = 'failed'","definition":"The subscriber responded with a non-2xx status, or the request timed out, or a connection error occurred"},{"domain_term":"subscriber URL","technical_term":"subscriberUrl: string parameter","definition":"The HTTP endpoint registered by the subscriber to receive webhook POST requests"}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are clear and Bird/MJ fully agree on Sprint 1 scope","Acceptance criteria map directly to Bird's ACs with no gaps","Sprint 1 vs Sprint 2 boundary is explicitly defined by both agents"],"low_confidence_areas":["Timeout behavior not explicitly stated by Bird — inferred as 'failed' from the 5-second rule; MJ did not contradict"],"assumptions":["Connection errors (DNS failure, connection refused) are treated as 'failed' with http_status_code null, same as timeout — neither agent specified this but it is the only reasonable behavior","No authentication or signing of webhook payloads is required in Sprint 1 — neither agent mentioned it"]}}
